I suspect it means eliminating all sugar and, as you say, stay away from all carbs veggies (other than peas, corn, and potatoes until I'm within 30 pounds of my weight. ### Shijin13 — 2014-04-01T19:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-review-help.10412/post-119268>

JNV read Hormones 101 and Hormones 102
[http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/](http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/)
[http://jackkruse.com/quantum-bio-10-hormone-102-drawn-to-light/](http://jackkruse.com/quantum-bio-10-hormone-102-drawn-to-light/)

You've got the TSH but T3 update and T4 total - don't tell the story. Good docs will run a Free T3 and Free T4, as well as Reverse T3. - this tells you how your Thyroid is making T4, and converting it to either Free T3 or Reverse T3.

As for carbs - stick to leafy greens, summer squashes. Peas are legumes so you should drop them too. Corn and potatoes should be kicked to the curb - and are why your trigs are so high. If you're going to have starchy carbs - limit them to fall/winter squashes/roots during fall - and in limited qtys.

Limit your fruit as well. if you want fruit consider wild edibles... such as wild raspberries, blackberries, blueberries, mulberries, gooseberries and crabapples...

> TESTOSTERONE FREE: 7.8 / 6.8 - 21.5 pg/mL
> SEX HORMONE BINDING GLOBULIN: 17.7 / 16.5 - 55.9 nmol/L
> ESTRADIOL: 39 / <11 - 52.5 pg / mL
> PSA, TOTAL (ULTRASEN): 0.242 NOTE:
> DEHYDROEPIANDROSTERONE: 1.820 / 0.630 to 4.700 ng/mL
>
> Background: Male late 40's WEIGHT 250 HEIGHT 5'6
>
> Levothyroxine (for thyroid) - .50 mg daily
> I am allergic to gluten
> I have had sleep apnea for 5+ years or so.

\=======================================================
1grain of Natural Thyroid from pigs=(T4-38mcg)+(T3-9mcg)=38+36=74mcg-T4 equivalent
1mcg-T3=4mcg-T4
9mcg-T3=36mcg-T4 equivalent

Consider recalculating your current dose of Levothyroxine=T4 converting it to equivalent NTH, and ask your doc for permission to use it
\===========================================================================
CORTISOL AM: 11.3 / 3.7 - 19.4 ug/dl
PREGNENOLONE: 20 /<121 ng/dL

Both are low.
Adjust your daily life so it is in agreement with Circadian Rhythm.
If you live above 40degree lattide supplement with 10000iu/day vit D
also A & K2

Get 4xsaliva test to figure out your current cortisol profile.

Likely you are also low in progesterone
Consider pregnenolone supplementation sufficient to raise it to
preg~180ng/dL
prog (1.4-4)ng/dL

After you get there re-evaluate thyroid panel
\============================================================================
You tested DHEA
get also DHEAs
most everybody is low on DHEAs, start with 50mg/day supplementation
aim at DHEAs(350-490) μg/dL
\============================================================================
You are low in Total Testosterone
you may want to check LH & FSH, specially if you want preserve fertility.
consider supplementation with testosterone and HCG.
there are two major types of testosterone supplementation,
transdermal and injectable
your goal is to keep TT, DHT, E2 at good levels
with SHBG~17 you want to raise TT to about 700-900ng/dL
to improve erections you want DHT(75-100)ng/dL
erection also heavily depend on E2 & progesterone levels
transdermal testosterone works better when it is thin (1%), the higher density may work but the higher the % the more problematic it becomes
thyroid problems affect skin permeability to testosterone
transdermal testosterone goes thru 5aR within skin, that converts testosterone to DHT, dihydro-testosterone
the thinner testosterone the more area must be used the higher DHT raises
it is all balancing act
injectable testosterone is surer way to raise testosterone, have its own set of problems
easiest to use and figure out proper dose is testosterone cypionate or enantathe
but injections should be done at least weekly, preferably EOD, E3D or 2x/week
Good starting dose for your SHBG level would be 40mg/EOD=140mg/week on average
sometimes combination of Cypionate & Androgel is need (Androgel-1% to raise DHT)
Androgel-1% alone tends to raise DHT sky high
newer ever ever ever use 5Ar inhibitors (finasteride, duosteride, Avodart, Proscar, Propecia, herbals containing such actions).

\===========================================================================

250# weight
get scale that measures body fat (Ozeri sold on Amazon)
aim at 10-15% body fat
you must loose lots of weight
at this time strenuous exercises may hurt you, but long hours of CT are your friend
being allergic to gluten helps you, skip also any carbohydrates that raise insulin and blood glucose
review Jacks Resistant Starch blog, make best use of it.
Practically most nuts are suspect that they are heat treated and therefore worthless as a source of resistant starch, it may be hard to follow that blog.
Keep in back pocket info on unmodified potato starch (4 tablespoons/day). Some claim good results.
There is a strong correlation between resistant starches and your high insulin and glucose.

\=============================================================================
CRP HIGH SENSITIVITY: 3.40 / <7.48 mg/L

fatty acids are highly influential in controlling inflammation
most popular are discussions of omega6/omega3 ratios.
Those discussions often are lacking enough information that would allow precise follow up testing
Also omega7 are responsible for inflammation management

Without getting into more details, safe way is to avoid all fish oil and krill oil supplementation
instead
divide in half your weekly allotment meats and make sure that for each pound of pork of beef you eat a found of oysters, shrimps and fish

Make dense broths (50/50 land animal/seafood) bones, cartilage.
Drink spring water

I am not a doctor.

Your #1 problem is to clarify your pituitary status.
Pituitary rules many processes that we talk about.
Eventually you should monitor all of your pituitary hormones for a period of time sufficient to figure out what kind help/intervention they need to steer your body in optimal way.
Until you are not clear on your pituitary,
any cortisol, thyroid, testosterone interventions are temporary and subject to drastic change.
Likely it may be wise to not take any supplements (over the counter) or medicines (prescription) unless specifically advised by the doctor who is helping you with your pituitary. Taking them on your own may obscure evaluation of your pituitary.

Since you have a doctor who figured out your pituitary problems,
consider sticking with him for your other heath problems.
Pituitary is one of the master switches, doctor who can figure out your pituitary have mostly proved himself to you.
So for now it would be a good idea to give that doctor enough time to fix you up best he can.
\=================================================

(Generally) You have number of ways going about finding another doctor.
Friendly general practitioner, most often MD but osteopath may be better. If he have open mind and allows you to go your way supporting it with scripts for medicine and tests, that may be a keeper.

Doctor who know more about natural healing, review Shijin13's webinar on how to find doctor. One of the ways would be to check with Genowa Diagnostics Laboratory asking them for names of doctors who order their labs.
Checking with local compounding pharmacy asking for docs ordering their products.
Best would be if you could hire one of the doctors that shows up on dr Kruses presentations.

Practically no doctor can help you without your cooperation.
So, it will be easier for you to take good advice if you have at least general understanding of where your problem may be. That is likely main reason why dr Kruse is educating all of us.
\=====================
Good Circadian Rhythm is one of the basics, you live or die because of it.
You will newer be able to correct anything, specially the extra 90# of fat that you are carrying around without improvements in Circadian Rhythm.

To support or reestablish good Circadian rhythm:

Live like people lived before invention of electricity, preferably 500-5000 years before that.

wakeup at dawn, right before sunrise (you have been previous 8 hrs in totally dark room)
barefoot and naked walk on grass or sandy beach (look up Caroline and our other girls, they love watching)
look at the sun 2-3 minutes (note when sun just rises it is not as bright as latter on)
look at bright part of the sky if cloudy (shuts off melatonin, reinforces cortisol)
do that snow, wind, rain or shine,
preferably
take shower in cold water
within 30-60 min from wakeup eat 50% of your daily food

lunch

dinner ends 4 hrs before bed time

sundown, no electric lights, specially in blue spectrum, red and amber are ok (camp fire), use flux.com to change brightness of computer screen
sleep within 10PM-8AM window

\====================

> proteins=(min 1/2 pound/day) of shellfish from mollusks and crustaceans, balance beef, pork, (birds eggs only),
> worst farm raised seafood is better than best grass fed beef

> Following dr Kruse, I would summarize diet as:
> No grains whatsoever, no potatoes & sugar. (But look up caveat, hot potato, cold potato, resistant starch)
> Carbohydrates -- only minimal amount and not in winter
> Protein=30% of daily caloric intake
> Fat=70% of daily caloric intake<<<<<<<<<<=============================
> Protein part=70%seafood(DHA) + 30%(pork, beef, no birds except eggs)(AA)
>
> To loose weight, from above cut out some fat, if hungry replace that with additional seafood.
> And maximize CT

Problems with fish oils.

1----it is commercial product that went thru number of processes before it got into bottle or gelcap.
contains extremely high amount of EPA in relation to DHA. Using fish/krill oils you will always have excess EPA.

2---- Some claim that the DHA in fish/kril oils is not the real DHA that we want, but it is missing some electrons (otherwise about the same), but we do care a lot about electrons their spin and inclination and coherence.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2)

3----large amounts of EPA + DHA (or any other omega3) will suppress omega6, specially AA.
omega3-resist inflammation
omega6-promotes inflammation
HS-CRP ->> = one of the inflammations indicators
we want omega6/omega3 in balance (to create inflammation/anti-inflammation balance)
that balance is evaluated many different ways
most known (and least clear) is O6/O3 ratio
least prone to counterfeit and obscurity is:

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
done at MayoClinic or LabCorp

best known to me is:

[http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/](http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/)
BodyBio Wellness Report
BodyBio Professional Report (Doctor's Report)
BodyBio Fatty Acid Report (Doctor's Report) <<<<<===================

\=============

Corrections are done mostly by manipulating diet.

\==========================================
There is a zillion of articles about fish and krill oils.
There is a possibility that all claims are correct for very narrow and specific applications.
All that is forgotten, and fish and krill oils flow is likely bigger than flow of statins.

Ed and Patricia Kane (fatty acids experts) are the lonely voice against indiscriminate fish/krill oil use.
But I go with what they say.

For what is worth, dr Kruse refered me to Patricia Kane as a good source of information on fatty acids. But dr Kruse still sells fish and krill oils, so take all this, digest it, and use what makes sense to you. Nothing is really cast in stone.

**BUT PER LEF.ORG CLAIMS, your high insulin and highish glucose could use some omega7 (and resistant starch),
but settle first your GH/pituitary issue.**

bodybio.com

Lipoprotein Particle Profile, DHEA-S, Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum, Free T3 and Free T4, as well as Reverse T3, Vitamin D3 - 25 hydroxy, IGFBP3, and a MRI for my pituitary (when it can be scheduled). She did say until we have the results for the MRI, nothing should be done in terms of hormone replacement therapy.

She is also going to put me on Armour Thyroid vs. Levothyroxine at my request.

She also referred me to a urologist. In doing her prostate exam, which I always absolutely hate, she felt a nodule. My PSA is great, so this should be interesting.

There is two way to tend to a gut flora, may be three.
1\. support and feed existing gut flora so it multiplies ----- eat proper food, resistant starch helps here
2\. eat food containing gut flora ---- sauerkraut, kimchi, raw food
3\. supplement with pills containing gut flora, butyrate pills, inulin, pre-biotics...

I am not sure what safe starch is.
Resistant starch is a starch that resist digestion by human body but is a food for beneficial bugs residing in our body.
Those bugs grow in number in presence of resistant starch.

Recently Ameer Rosic had webinar on this topic. Mostly I remember potatoes, rice, beans, plantans, green bananas and unmodified potato starch.
Unmodified potato starch is ready to eat from the bag as long as is not heated.
Potato and rice are cooked as usual, when hot they contain starch that we here do not care about,
but,
when you cool them to below 50F previously digestible starch converts to resistant starch.
German potato salad is good representative of how to get this type of starch in the meal.

I understand that all this may not be exactly what dr Kruse is preaching but he supports Ameer so it may be acceptable alternative.

Sometimes it is hard for me to figure what dr Kruse supports and what not.
Often it is on case by case basis, same key can be the latest cat's meau and again same key can open the door too hell.
So whatever action you decide to take, it is all you. Just make sure to pick the correct bread crumbs and you are going to be ok.

Likely the best would be to do testing to monitor progress and figure out where you need some help at given time.
glucose
insulin
stool analysis

# Thread: True Thirst?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2014-04-01T11:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-119194>

Hey,

So in today's world, many people who are cellularly dehydrated don't feel dehydrate, ie don't feel thirsty. This is probably because the environment we live in is messing with our bodies signals and our bodies general health is usually bad, with messed up hormones, etc. This all means it cannot sense the dehydration.
Or maybe once your body is kicked out of using water for energy and is forced to use ATP, it craves ATP instead, as this is what it can use for energy right now.

Either way our true thirst signal to refill out nano-tubes is absent. I can think of 2 ways to get this back. The first is grounding and sunlight. Jack said this and there has been some anecdotal evidence of these forums.

The second is fasting. It is very common when water fasting to experience a dry mouth. On the things I have read about water fasting it says that the dry mouth goes away after a few weeks (or however long it takes) and then true hunger returns (you lose your hunger on the fast). I did not personally experience this but I was doing it wrong. All the other fasting symptoms (detox) go away at this point.

So I think what is happening there is the fast awakens your true thirst by forcing you into ketosis and forcing you to use water as an energy source again. After a few weeks when you have drunk enough water to refill your nano tubes, the dry mouth stops, detox is finished and hunger returns.

I have recently dropped my morning meal and am now just eating an evening one. This has cut my calories roughly in half. I have upped my water intake by ~1.5L. Upon doing this I noticed my body started doing the same reactions it did when I last did water fasting (weakness, dizziness, dry mouth, sore throat, flem, bad breath, etc). So my body is treating this 24hr between food thing like it does a proper fast. Anyway I have decreased my food intake (incl. protein) and increased my water so I should be feeling more hydrated however I have been having more dry mouth and the top of my throat is soar and rough every night. This indicates true thirst is coming back somewhat as I'm feeling more dehydrated. Or it could be that I am more dehydrated because my body is using more of that water I'm drinking as energy.

Anyway my question is, can you force your body into the PPP / using water as it's main source of energy if you give it no other option? Ie, take away the food so it cannot use ATP and replace it with extra water.

Part 2, does hypothyroid have some benefits with regards to entering the PPP? As in having a lower core body temperature which would improve water chemistry.

### seanb4 — 2014-04-14T12:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121411>

Update: Lasted about a week with the lower calories but couldn't maintain it. Started to eat more at my main meal and fasting symptoms went away. I think this could be because my body was now relying more on ATP instead of water for energy, it could also be because the extra food was causing more inflammation in my body via the stress response I get after eating.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-14T12:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121413>

There is a theory out there and it is way out there that the body realises on a subconscious level that the water is no good and reins in the thirst mechanism. I can´t quite believe it as it is anti survival but I put it out there to discuss.

### seanb4 — 2014-04-14T12:43:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121414>

Maybe, if it gets confused. EMFs could be causing the confusion along with other things...

I think it could be that once you switch to using ATP for energy, instead of water, your body starts to seek ATP (carbs) because that's what it is currently using for energy and it switches your bodies instincts to look for that instead as it assumes your enviroment has abundant ATP but not much water.

### Jonathin — 2014-04-14T18:05:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121439>

ATP is necessary to unfold proteins so that they can be hydrolyzed by water. I am not sure if ATP is necessary once they are in that state however it seems that once any amount of dehydration occurs ATP is once again needed. It seems like a chicken and egg thing - there is no beginning and end but all factors need to be in place for things to work.

### seanb4 — 2014-04-15T19:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-121684>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=121439)
>
> ATP is necessary to unfold proteins so that they can be hydrolyzed by water. I am not sure if ATP is necessary once they are in that state however it seems that once any amount of dehydration occurs ATP is once again needed. It seems like a chicken and egg thing - there is no beginning and end but all factors need to be in place for things to work.

I assume that a certain (small) amount of ATP would be need to maintain the upkeep for proteins hydrolyzed by water. When proteins are recycled wouldn't they need to be re-hydrolyzed? But yeah food would be necessary anyway as building blocks for the body.

Still would like to here peoples opinions of being able to force your way into the PPP by eating keto, lower calories, but decent water. You would be supplying your body with minimal ATP but abundant water, if it's smart it should have to use the PPP or die...

### nonchalant — 2015-06-06T21:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/true-thirst.10417/post-166856>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=121439)
>
> ATP is necessary to unfold proteins so that they can be hydrolyzed by water. I am not sure if ATP is necessary once they are in that state however it seems that once any amount of dehydration occurs ATP is once again needed. It seems like a chicken and egg thing - there is no beginning and end but all factors need to be in place for things to work.

Well, every morning you have cortisol running amok, stripping electrons from the proteins in collagen. The proteins would become less hydrophilic. I think their shape would change without the extra charges.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-02T19:33:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-england.10421/post-119436>

Actually it was........when I was a fat ass........I loved golf. Seeing your pic reminded of that love.......I gave it up because of all the shit I found on my clothes after i played. I think it was due to my golf passion. In the UK I bet you dont manicure things as americans want..........our wants of the visual beauty have ruined my love of the sport.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-02T19:44:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-cambridge-england.10421/post-119441>

> [davidgolfpro said:](/goto/post?id=119227)
>
> Hi everybody,
> I started resetting my leptin today, and am very excited at this method of losing weight and optimal health. I have never been so excited about nutrition in my life! Thanks!

My uncle is a keen golfer. He plays at Moor Park. Unfortunately, he now has several medical problems, including diabetes, and found his form went after he was recovering from a couple of big operations. This, apparently, meant he was no longer a "fox" but a "rabbit" (hope I have the names right). By Christmas his handicap had improved again. He ended up going to the foxes' dinner and the rabbits' dinner. His wife said, "Surely, you can't go to both dinners; you must be one or the other?" He said, "I've been invited to both, and I'm attending any dinner I can get!"

# Thread: PPP
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppp.10425/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melanie Procter — 2014-04-02T04:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppp.10425/post-119351>

I am very interested in the topic of glutathione production. I think I am one of those former cross fitters who beat the crap out of myself and am on the mend. I am truly the classic case. I thus was attracted to Dr. Kruse's interview with Ben Greenfield they did on Ben's show.

I listened to certain sections of the interview over and over. Jack was talking of the 24-36 month window to get into the PPP. I felt all of us athletes including Ben thinking, "What? Wait! Huh?, What?"

So, what's the fastest is track? If we want to get on the road to this direction, do we need to stay in ketosis all the time? Or can we have a once a week carb fest as suggested by Tim Ferris in the "Four Hour Body"? Can we have a few glasses of wine on occasion and still get "there"?

Perhaps I need to search the forum better. Maybe there is a PPP Rx blog out there. I read PPP but am looking for the RX.

I thank you all for your support. I am trying to read the blogs as fast as possible so I can catch up with everyone here. I look forward to the day when I can support others on the forum as you all have been supporting me the past week or two since I have joined.

Mel

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-04-02T11:16:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppp.10425/post-119383>

if leptin is back on track, then start doing CT protocol whilst increasing fat consumption ala the leptin Rx, because PPP is a winter pathway that cold exposure has the key for...
also it revolves a lot around sleep and circadian cycles as outlined on Jack's pages

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-04-02T11:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppp.10425/post-119384>

in MY opinion a carb fest is a waste of time, because it will bounce you out of ketosis and take the old opportunistic glucose pathway instead. because you are becoming fat adapted you will become increasingly insulin sensitive.
if you have a carb fest you are signalling summertime again and losing the benefits of PPP

# Thread: First Experiment Ketosis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### persistence — 2014-04-02T21:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119458>

I decided to do an experiment with how easily I could go into ketosis and wanted some feedback on my results.

Yesterday I set aside about four hours before lunch and had nothing but tea with either monounsaturated fat or saturated coconut oil added. I was adding about two tablespoons per cup, so a decent amount of oil. I would guess I drank about six cups of tea over the four hours. Oh, almost forgot I also had some Hershey's Unsweetened Baking Bar chocolate, which is 100% cacao. I think there is no sugar added on that, although they do list some carbohydrate on label. It's quite sour to the taste, so I don't think there is any substantial sugar or starch.

At the end of four hours I have my ketones up to 1.6 mmol/L. I guess technically that means I am already in ketsosis at that point? I ate a lunch of fish and vegetables, no starches, and ended up peeking my ketones around 2.5 mmol/L about two hours after finishing that lunch.

What I did notice during this experiment:

1) My steady state glucose fell from the 95 to 110 level to 80 to 90.

2) I had some sense of energy leveling out and not being so dramatically up and down.

By the next morning ketones were at 0.2 mmol/L. Would it be typical to see them disappear so quickly?

I would like to do some experiments going into ketosis and out. For one, I want to test what happens to my glucose when I try to enter ketosis rapidly in morning, before eating breakfast, by drinking some tea with lots of oil. (See other thread I started for this.) Does anyone see any harm in going in and out of ketosis frequently?

### Dali Dula — 2014-04-03T00:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119509>

Fat bomb breakfast - heat in small saucepan
1tbs each of the following:
MCT oil
Coconut oil
Ghee
Heavy whipping cream

In a big mug put two eggs (duck if you have them)
Some cinnamon and vanilla
3tbs unprocessed cocoa powder
Add boiling water to fill mug half way
Whisk up cocoa, egg, hot water
Add melted fat, whisk till frothy
I am not hungry for at least 8 hours after this.
If you want no protein at all use just egg yolk instead of whole eggs.
You could do this with coffee a la Bulletproof, but the cocoa is great and brings some resistant starch to the game.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-04-03T01:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119511>

Generally speaking, not eating anything at all is the fastest way to naturally get into ketosis. Coconut oil will definitely boost up blood ketone levels, which is probably why you're seeing ketones go up after consuming said meals.

You've talked a lot about your glucose management issues. Do you normally see dawn phenomena? If so, that could explain the low morning ketone readings, and you should test again 4 hours after waking to see if ketones start to rise again.

Personally, I generally wake up in the 0.5-0.8mM range, eat a fat-only meal for breakfast, and then get up to 1.0-1.5mM 4 hours post waking, and then usually hover anywhere from 1.0-2.0mM all the way until dinner. If there's any eating in between, it's almost always fat or very low carb/protein. After a regular day's dinner (roughly 100g protein), I'd be back to around 0.6-0.8mM 4 hours after the meal. This is all without consumption of particularly ketogenic fats. On days that I do specifically consume any form of MCTs, afternoon readings can get above 3.0mM.

I should note that I tend to view clinically defined ketosis (>0.5mM BHB) as a leading indicator of an underlying state of high fat oxidation, rather than a be-all-end-all goal. The goal is to get your cells to be burning fat, and "going in and out of ketosis" really means switching between different energetic substrates. My opinion is that sustained fatty acid oxidation is probably something you want if you're trying to reverse disease. Ketones can act as the switch that tells your cells to burn fat, but aren't the underlying mechanism for optimal mitochondrial function as far as I know.

Wow, you are an animal.

Some background here is that I may have some kind of fat malabsorption syndrome. So I am at the point where I need to do measured experiments with different types of fats and discover which ones do I absorb. This morning using monounsaturated fats wasn't moving my ketone needle at all. This afternoon, it only took two cups with coconut oil, and my ketones were at 2.5 mmol/L. I did primary research and found a study that says poly oils produce 2.5 times as many ketones as saturated fats. I would never eat poly oils, but it still makes it all worth testing some more. I never bought my ketone meter thinking that it would end up being used to diagnose my fat absorption defects, but it sure is going to be handy for exactly that.

### persistence — 2014-04-03T05:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119557>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=119511)
>
> Generally speaking, not eating anything at all is the fastest way to naturally get into ketosis. Coconut oil will definitely boost up blood ketone levels, which is probably why you're seeing ketones go up after consuming said meals.

Okay, great, but other oils - in absence of a large starchy carb meal - should also produce some ketones? I want the details because I have severe aborption issues, so for me getting ketosis to work without starvation may be tricky.

> You've talked a lot about your glucose management issues. Do you normally see dawn phenomena? If so, that could explain the low morning ketone readings, and you should test again 4 hours after waking to see if ketones start to rise again.

I have a huge dawn effect. I can go from glucose 80 on rising to 130 without food on some days.

Part of this is maybe cortisol getting out of control (I don't know). But surely part of it is I have defective liver glucose metabolism and something in the self-regulation system is broken. The liver is producing too much glucose.

But I have noticed that entering ketosis resets my glucose setpoint. This afternoon, I entered ketosis for the second time, and almost instantly my first glucose test after entering ketosis is at 83.

So my insight for further experiment: maybe a prediabetic can try to enter ketosis again when getting up in morning, and use this to reset / alter the shape of the dawn effect, and get glucose down lower, faster.

> Personally, I generally wake up in the 0.5-0.8mM range, eat a fat-only meal for breakfast, and then get up to 1.0-1.5mM 4 hours post waking, and then usually hover anywhere from 1.0-2.0mM all the way until dinner. If there's any eating in between, it's almost always fat or very low carb/protein. After a regular day's dinner (roughly 100g protein), I'd be back to around 0.6-0.8mM 4 hours after the meal. This is all without consumption of particularly ketogenic fats. On days that I do specifically consume any form of MCTs, afternoon readings can get above 3.0mM.

This is a great data point for me. You are saying it takes your body about four hours to digest the fats you eat for breakfast and start to register additional ketones? I was expecting my ketones to rise within one or two hours of eating fats in morning. If it takes four hours, then that doesn't work for purposes of adjusting the dawn effect.

> I should note that I tend to view clinically defined ketosis (>0.5mM BHB) as a leading indicator of an underlying state of high fat oxidation, rather than a be-all-end-all goal. The goal is to get your cells to be burning fat, and "going in and out of ketosis" really means switching between different energetic substrates. My opinion is that sustained fatty acid oxidation is probably something you want if you're trying to reverse disease. Ketones can act as the switch that tells your cells to burn fat, but aren't the underlying mechanism for optimal mitochondrial function as far as I know.

Which diseases do you think it reverses besides diabetes?

What do you think is the underlying mechanism for mitochondrial function?

### yewwei.tan — 2014-04-03T07:05:26+0300

>

I was expecting my ketones to rise within one or two hours of eating fats in morning. If it takes four hours, then that doesn't work for purposes of adjusting the dawn effect.
>
>
>
> Which diseases do you think it reverses besides diabetes?
>
> What do you think is the underlying mechanism for mitochondrial function?

I think eating fats allow for more ketone production, so long as your body can break them down quickly enough. If I'm not wrong, ketone production is induced by:

1.  low blood glucose relative to energetic demands
2.  more acetyl-CoA than the TCA cycle can deal with

MCTs, are then the "most ketogenic" fat mainly because they get shunted straight into the bloodstream without much processing at all, where they are rapidly beta-oxidised to form acetyl-CoA.

So you should keep up with the coconut oil strategy (which is 14% Capric and Caprillic acid). However, I don't know if your fat malabsorption issues would come into play here. For example, if bile production is too low, then you're not going to be able to metabolise a fat like olive oil fast enough to get you into ketosis faster (than not consuming the oil). Again, MCTs side-step this mechanism and end up right in the blood ready for beta-oxidation. In the meantime, you should probably investigate the cause of said fat malabsorption.

\===

For me personally, I actually see the same blood ketone pattern even through fasting; I was experimenting with not eating until 12pm (5-hour post waking), and ketones basically continually rose through the morning. But it's always lower in the morning than it is in the afternoon, and this pattern has been consistent for 4 months now (I transitioned fully from a low-carb diet to fully keto in about September of last year -- so about 6 full months).

It's more likely that activity jacks up my ketone levels more so than anything else. For example, it's pretty common for me to see ketones go from 1.0mM to 2.0+mM over the course of some simple low volume resistance training (example: 3x5 chinups, 5x5 weighted dips, 3x10 accessory pulling movement). Endurance activity doesn't spike ketone levels as high for me, but that's more likely because they're being used up pretty quickly after they're produced.

\===

There's lots of "mainstream" research regarding ketones and their therapeutic effect on cancer. But basically we're just getting a reverse Warburg effect happening, which cures tons of diseases (CPC8 is a good read in this regard)

All I'm saying is that the things that you need to do to stay in prolonged ketosis provides mitochondria with the appropriate building blocks for energy production, and makes the cellular environment more favourable. But let's not forget that being in ketosis is just one way to effect that environment, and isn't exactly the the "make-or-break" factor. Again CPC8 goes into some detail, in particular the sections '**The Mechanism**', '**The Mechanism Origin**', '**RETURNING TO THE MECHANISM OF ONCOGENESIS: GEEK FEST**', and of course the section '**WHY KETOSIS MAKES SENSE WITH A LOW REDOX/DELTA PSI** ' -- [http://jackkruse.com/cpc-8-quantum-brain-cancer/](http://jackkruse.com/cpc-8-quantum-brain-cancer/)

How long after your last meal does it take before ketones are below 0.5?

>

>

I'm saying that regardless of whether I eat fat or not, my ketones start out low, and then rise progressively through the day to the same degree. (No carbs or significant amounts of protein of course) In other words, I don't need to eat to get ketones levels up, but if I did eat some fat, I'd still be in ketosis anyway.

With MCT, it's very fast. After even 1 tablespoon of NOW foods MCT in the morning (which should be 1:1 ratio of C8:C6), I'd go from 0.5mM to >1.5mM within 15 mins, and then usually settling around the 1.0-1.5mM mark an hour later. If I take MCT in the afternoon, I can easily spike levels to 3 or 4mM, which is usually accompanied by lots of jitteriness

These days, I'm doing a mostly 2 meal a day system, starting the day with a cup of cocoa with butter and MCT oil, and then a small handful of nuts. This gets me ketotic really quickly and I can basically stay there for a long time. My subjective metric in this case is mental alertness -- I'm a computer programmer, and can usually tell when my brain is "working off ketones" so to speak.

Ketones tank very fast after eating any form of protein or carbs thought, which I think can be attributed to insulin secretion (which starts the moment you put food into your mouth). Typically it's like 0.2-0.3mM right after, and then takes 4 hours to climb back into the >0.8mM range.

\----

I can't see how something like pure C8 and C6 MCT would raise LDL, and I wouldn't necessarily say that high LDL particle count is a bad thing -- it requires the context of your other lab values, so you know how best to interpret that reading.

Its also why some need a higher calorie count that (Gasp) may include more carbs. I loose weight when I'm eating more food (Its totally and electron thing) and I gain when I eat less. Why does this happen? B/c women carry on the line. we're so sensitive to epigenetic signals so we can inform our progeny. I think this is why HCG works for some women and not as well as others, given their particular redox potential and their environmental influences. I think Jeremy highlighted this well in the Q&A - not everyone can follow Jack's recommendations to a T, b/c of their own separate N=1. Some of us can and some of us can't be zero carb. Some of us need different combinations of foods to make things work for us.

### persistence — 2014-04-03T22:18:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119657>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=119591)
>
> I'm saying that regardless of whether I eat fat or not, my ketones start out low, and then rise progressively through the day to the same degree. (No carbs or significant amounts of protein of course) In other words, I don't need to eat to get ketones levels up, but if I did eat some fat, I'd still be in ketosis anyway.
>
> With MCT, it's very fast. After even 1 tablespoon of NOW foods MCT in the morning (which should be 1:1 ratio of C8:C6), I'd go from 0.5mM to >1.5mM within 15 mins, and then usually settling around the 1.0-1.5mM mark an hour later. If I take MCT in the afternoon, I can easily spike levels to 3 or 4mM, which is usually accompanied by lots of jitteriness

What do you attribute the jitteriness to? I find too much MCT Oil makes my heart beat too hard. It's not a pleasant sensation. It feels like metabolism is upregulated and not controlled well.

> Ketones tank very fast after eating any form of protein or carbs thought, which I think can be attributed to insulin secretion (which starts the moment you put food into your mouth). Typically it's like 0.2-0.3mM right after, and then takes 4 hours to climb back into the >0.8mM range.

What we really need is a 24x7 continuous glucose / ketone monitor so we can quickly grasp the speed and magnitude of different dietary inputs.

> I can't see how something like pure C8 and C6 MCT would raise LDL, and I wouldn't necessarily say that high LDL particle count is a bad thing -- it requires the context of your other lab values, so you know how best to interpret that reading.

High LDL cholesterol is not necessarily bad. But high \*particle counts\* correlates in the research very well to risk of cardiac death. In fact the highest risk group in some studies is people with both low cholesterol and high LDL particle counts. Track the number of particles, not the total cholesterol.

There is famous research by a guy named Goldstein that demonstrates how - in some animals and people - high cholesterol or fat intakes in diet can cause downregulation of LDL receptors. If your body is overproducing LDL, it cannot then be cleared from the blood. I could post some of his old papers if you are interested, but he has one discussion from 1984 that is classic where he compares rabbits to rats. Feed the rabbits high fat in diet and their LDL receptors downregulate and they become hyperlipidemic. Feed the rats the same diet and the LDL receptors do not downregulate and the LDL level in blood stays normal in spite of high dietary fat. Goldstein speculates some humans are like the rabbits and some are like the rats. Note that the above is NOT about familial hypercholesterolemia. That is a genetic condition where the LDL receptors never work correctly in the first place. There is no downregulation involved.

That's why I think blanket statements about "saturated fat is good" or "saturated fat is bad" are not meaningful. They can be either, for a given individual, based on how they process fats.

I don't know of any tests that would identify whether a given human is like the rat or the rabbit. Probably there is a way to test it, but I am guessing it is done at some obscure research level. I would love to know about such a test.

### persistence — 2014-04-03T22:24:13+0300

All interesting questions.

If your ketones are the result of intentional fasting, then eating carbs is going to shut down ketosis. That I understand.

What if you are ingesting MCT Oil and that is getting shunted to the liver to produce ketones. Now you ingest some carbs. Is the MCT Oil going to simply stop being digested and not produce ketones while glucose is being used? I find that hard to believe. Any citations anyone?

If in fact you can force ketone production \*while eating carbs\*, then you get the basis for my original observation: maybe the presence of significant ketones in the blood when eating carbs downregulates your glucose production.

### persistence — 2014-04-03T22:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119660>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=119642)
>
> AMEN Gretchen. I know for me when eating almost no carbs I still could not get the strips to go above small, so frustrating.

First, buy a real Ketone meter. Those pee-pee strips are not good enough for detailed experiment.

Second, try to take a lunch with no starchy carbs, then set aside the next four hours for an experiment. Try to take two tablespoons of MCT Oil every 30 minutes, and then test your ketone level every hour. I think you are going to find that with an easily-absorbed oil like MCT you will jump into ketosis, dramatically, and quickly. For me it was like being five years old and making my first jump into the shallow end of the pool, then getting out and feeling good I had survived.

I think you then build on those little experiments. It's like learning scales on the piano in order to later be able to play music.

For me, I am going to hire ketogenic nutritionists to coach me on this. I want to be completely in control of this and become very knowledgeable about different fats and how they process.

Ha, I think I'm getting my terms mixed up here. I really meant to just casually mention the Warburg hypothesis (not warburg effect) and then say that being in ketosis is one way to improve cellular function (CPC8 goes into all the detail).

For more mainstream biology work, just catch all the recent interviews with Dominic D'agostino, who basically talks about everything in CPC8.

\====

With that said, this is one of those cases whereby Wikipedia gets things really wrong (by not providing us with the full details). Here's a blog post on Jimmy Moore's site, where he brings in great researchers like Thomas Seyfried to comment on the studies which that Wiki article is based off -- [http://livinlavidalowcarb.com/blog/do-ketones-fuel-cancer-the-low-carb-experts-respond/10124](http://livinlavidalowcarb.com/blog/do-ketones-fuel-cancer-the-low-carb-experts-respond/10124)

Feed the rabbits high fat in diet and their LDL receptors downregulate and they become hyperlipidemic. Feed the rats the same diet and the LDL receptors do not downregulate and the LDL level in blood stays normal in spite of high dietary fat. Goldstein speculates some humans are like the rabbits and some are like the rats. Note that the above is NOT about familial hypercholesterolemia. That is a genetic condition where the LDL receptors never work correctly in the first place. There is no downregulation involved.
>
> That's why I think blanket statements about "saturated fat is good" or "saturated fat is bad" are not meaningful. They can be either, for a given individual, based on how they process fats.
>
> I don't know of any tests that would identify whether a given human is like the rat or the rabbit. Probably there is a way to test it, but I am guessing it is done at some obscure research level. I would love to know about such a test.

Me personally, I'm really a luddite (which is why I work as a programmer -- you can't defend yourself from what you don't understand). I don't think we need continuous monitoring of ketones or blood glucose to make good improvements in our health, provided that your already motivated to make changes.

ItsTheWoo comes off as one whiny blogger, but ignore the vernacular and she sometimes makes lots of sense -- [http://itsthewooo.blogspot.com/2014/03/uber-ketosis-not-necessary-and-possibly.html](http://itsthewooo.blogspot.com/2014/03/uber-ketosis-not-necessary-and-possibly.html) <= that's a post talking about why "extreme" levels of ketosis may not be necessary, and that "being a fat burner" is really what you're after.

In other words, use ketosis as an indicator that you are a fat burner, and not the be-all-end-all goal in and off itself. That's personally why I spend the money measuring my ketones -- not to try and best my last reading, but because if I see that those readings are diverging from my usual pattern, then I know something is wrong, and can better start the diagnostic process. **(Via Negativa)**

\====

Regarding LDL-P, we're in agreement that it's important, and Peter Attia has a great series regarding cholesterol -- [http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/the-straight-dope-on-cholesterol-part-vi](http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/the-straight-dope-on-cholesterol-part-vi)

I will still say that it's one number, and I don't know your particular LPL-P value, and how that value has fluctuated over time. And again, it needs to be taken in context with the rest of your lab values.

\====

Finally, when I say I get "jittery" when my blood ketones go above 3.0mM, I don't experience any heart palpitations or much negative physical symptoms. I just have a huge urge to engage in physical activity, and my mind can get very focused on very repetitive activities. I have no idea about the mechanism of such observations other than "it's the ketones!".

But the general idea still holds: if you can find the right oil and the right dose, taking that on its own after a non starchy meal for several hours should put you strongly in ketosis and you should be able to measure that with a meter. I had no problems getting ketone readings of 1 to 2.5 mmol/L.

### Josh — 2014-04-05T02:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-experiment-ketosis.10432/post-119849>

From Lyle McDonald's 1990's manual for ketogenic diets....

> In starvation, diabetes, sugar levels under strain
> You need fuel to keep going saving glucose for your brain
> Ketone bodies, Ketone bodies, both acetoacetate
> And its partner on reduction, 3-hydroxybutyrate.
> Glucagon’s up, with low glucose, insulin is down in phase
> Fatty acids mobilised by hormone-sensitive lipase
> Ketone bodies, Ketone bodies, all start thus from white fat cell
> Where through lack of glycerol-P, TG making’s down as well.
> Fatty acyl, CoA level, makes kinase phosphorylate
> Acetyl-CoA carboxy-lase to its inactive state
> Ketone bodies, Ketone bodies, because glucagon they say
> Also blocks carboxylation, lowers Malonyl-CoA.
> Malonyl-CoAs a blocker of the key CPT-1
> Blocking’s off so now the shuttle into mito’s is begun
> Now we’ve ß oxidation, now we’ve acetyl-CoA
> But what’s to stop it’s oxidation via good old TCA?
> In starvation, glucose making, stimulating PEP CK
> Uses oxaloacetic, also lost another way
> Ketone bodies, what is odd is that the oxidation state
> Also favours the reduction of OA to make malate.
> OA’s low now, citrate synthase, thus loses activity
> So the flux into the cycle cuts off (temporarily)
> Ketone bodies, Ketone bodies situation thus is this
> Acetyl-CoA’s now pouring into Ketogenesis.
> It’s a tricky little pathway, it’s got HMG-CoA
> In effect it’s condensation in a head-to-tailish way
> Ketone bodies, Ketone bodies, note the ratio of the pair
> Is controlled by NAD to NADH everywhere.
> Don’t despise them, they’re good fuels for your muscles, brain and heart
> When you’re bodies overloaded though, that’s when your troubles start
> Ketone bodies, ketone bodies, make acetone, lose CO2
> You can breath those out, but watch out - acidosis does for you!
> © “The Biochemists’ Songbook, 2nd ed.” Harold Baum. London: Taylor and Francis Publishers,

# Thread: Morning sunlight can do what I been telling you it can.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-sunlight-can-do-what-i-been-telling-you-it-can.10443/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-04T01:50:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-sunlight-can-do-what-i-been-telling-you-it-can.10443/post-119688>

**Study: Exposure to morning sunlight linked to lower BMI.**
[NBC News](http://mailview.bulletinhealthcare.com/mailview.aspx?m=2014040301ama&r=1772594-ac34&l=027-b4a&t=c) (4/2, Aleccia) reports on its website that according to a new study from Northwestern University’s Feinberg School of Medicine, people exposed to natural light earlier in the day had a lower BMI than those exposed to light in the afternoon. The study, published in the journal PLOS ONE, suggests that bright light in the morning helps synchronize “circadian rhythms that also regulate energy balance, including hormones such as melatonin, which regulate sleep and weight cycles and affect obesity.”

The [Los Angeles Times](http://mailview.bulletinhealthcare.com/mailview.aspx?m=2014040301ama&r=1772594-ac34&l=028-14c&t=c) (4/3, Kaplan) reports in its coverage that the volunteers in the study “tended to be night owls” who went to bed after midnight and rose before 9 am, and 58% reported a BMI of 24 or lower. The researchers found that MLiT (“mean light timing above threshold”), the measurement of light exposure, was the one variable in the study which correlated to BMI. The article notes that “grants from various branches of the National Institutes of Health” funded the study.

[HealthDay](http://mailview.bulletinhealthcare.com/mailview.aspx?m=2014040301ama&r=1772594-ac34&l=029-cfd&t=c) (4/3, Gordon) also covers the story.

### Shijin13 — 2014-04-04T02:33:47+0300

# Thread: What are your favorite March 2013 Q&A take aways
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kyrakitty — 2014-04-04T10:42:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119752>

These are mine:

Biology doesn't happen on the scale of DNA and RNA. It happens on the scale of proteins, it happens on the molecular scale of how bonds rotate and move when the electromagnetic spectrum that they are in changes. The electromagnetic signal that you are giving off, and the environment is giving you, constantly changes. The problem is that you are not aware of those unconscious movements. Proteins have an emergent property (a novel property that unpredictably comes from a combination of two simpler constituents) and the problem is we're not aware of it. Every protien has a hydration cell.

-   How does collagen form?
-   How does it electrify?
-   What functions does it have?

Above is the key to reduce my medical bills for sure.

Target goal and untapped road to optimal: Expand your collagen and water network in your body to help your mitochondria heal.

### caroline — 2014-04-04T11:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119756>

I think that Jeremy is so inspiring ..... understatement of the century! Jack and Jeremy are an awesome team .....two sides of the same coin. I have to listen again .....there was just too much to take on board.

Clearly they create quantum magic together .....and hugely enrich each other's life ......what a blessing for both of them.

I suspect we will owe Jeremy a great deal of gratitude over the next months .......as Dr. K. is able to scale this down for us non science geeks! It was so neat to see how Jeremy can scale this down to very simple terms and yet, he clearly gets every single nuance of what Dr. k. is saying and how it relates to him and his life.

I loved his honesty and simplicity and how he can say -" I really don't understand what Jack is saying - it is way over my head" but this is what I think.......

An important takeaway - Jeremy said to "Just start where you are at and don't compare yourself to anyone else"
"start small" "make every touch count" and every experience ...and carve an intention.
"Trust implicitly" "have passion and fall in love with an idea" "build a message and be patient"
"one size doesn't fit all"

Thankyou to Jeremy for sharing some of his story .....I sincerely hope we get to hear lots more soon.

I thought I was doing a lot of this stuff ...but he made me realise that I am not .......
I always wish I was smarter and had more money etc. et. etc.
I waste too much time wishing when I should be doing more ........ I don't think I do all that bad .....but he certainly made me realise that I can and should do a lot better to make the most of my life and all the things that I do have.

Therefore - I am carving a new intention .....now that I know better - I will do better!

Thanks Jeremy!!!

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-04T12:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119761>

Yes, shit I forgot Jeremy. I am going to take up yoga because of Jeremy! His collegan story blew me away and stretching the canvas can be done for free if one doesn't have money to spend on classes so I REALLY LOVE THIS.

### caroline — 2014-04-04T12:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119765>

Yes yoga - me too ...... it makes so much sense to learn to breathe properly and just sit quietly.

How is it we can learn so, so much realy hugely important stuff from someone so young???

we all let "things" define us ..... Jeremy doesn't let anything define him - not his disease - not anything .......he just figures out how to do what he wants to do, what he needs to do for him .....and then he does it.... or, at least, he gives it a darn good shot.

He makes everything in his life count ..... just WOW!

I honestly and truly believe what JK said ..... Jeremy is what drew him to the Gulf ..... he didn't know it at the time but he sure knows it now! and in spite of all he and his family went thru to make such a huge, life changing move ....and look at the wonderful things that have happened.....

How could you not believe in quantum magic and quantum entanglement??

I live this every single day......

When your hands and heart are open - you do receive gifts......amazing gifts! life altering gifts!

Look at all the amazing people that come into our life here .........because of Jack kruse .........I learn so much here every day - sometimes silly stuff, sometimes funny stuff, lots of great music, I learned that I loved dresses for a deep reason - more than I bargained for! and let's not forget all the QED and the brain pain!!!
I learn how people can be so strong in the face of so much adversity in their life - and I earn we can all be, at least a comfort and a support to each other - and offer words of encouragement and suggestions on our journey to an optimal life.......and this is all because Dr. k. has a story to share - and is willing to take the time to try and get us all to understand what he has learned.......

This is a massive undertaking ...... a labour of love......

Thanks is just never enough...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119817>

Oddly this ties in with the March 2013 webinar especially with how Jack talked about throwing down 3 webinars and enough blogs to write a book cuz he was in the zone/in the flow:

Dave Asprey's is the guest in the next Optimal webinar and in his recent podcast [#109 Steven Kotler and The Rise of Superman](http://www.bulletproofexec.com/109-steven-kotler-and-the-rise-of-superman-podcast/) – What was discussed was very similar to the March 2013 webinar only Steven doesn't use much of the quantum language and IMO I bet he would connect more dots if he was exposed more to it as discussed here on this site.

Anyway, the Guest Steven discusses being "In the Flow" at one point they made a comparison to how silicon valley is so successful due to the flow necessary for all the hardware aka semiconduction and how really successful organizations and people spend more time In the Flow.

Steven also talks about being soo sick with lyme and basically on his way to dying then a friend dragged him to the beach to surf. They had to assist him to the car, out of the car and onto the surf board cuz he couldn't walk on his own. He began to rapidly reverse his lyme disease simply by surfing... hmmm (Sun + grounding = improved redox) but this seemingly is overlooked verbally the healing he received due to improved redox. It is implied but not like we discuss it here.

Steven has a project called the flow genome project. It would be cool if he added the quantam side of things we all know of from Jack. I bet it would take his flow genome project exactly where he envisioned it would be.

Anyway, below is off one of the pages on the flow genome project so that individuals can learn about breaking the code to their own flow profile (n=1):

First it says: Take five minutes to fiund out which flow profile best matches who YOU are. Use the results to tune up your life and share with friends!

1) When I am in Flow, I am most often (pick one below)...

-   outside in nature
-   in a crowd with friends
-   someplace quite and reflective

You pick one of the above and it takes you on a series of questions for you to answer to learn more about how you can increase the amount of flow in your life! If you want to take the flow profile "test" here is the link

[http://www.flowgenomeproject.co/flowprofile/](http://www.flowgenomeproject.co/flowprofile/) (they ask for personal info address and phone number before they give you your flow profile, that's kinda lame,... so you may not want to try this if you don't want them to have your info). I did it and it had many accurate points to consider.

More excerpts from Steven's website:

**What is The Flow State**: Flow states, peak experiences, in the zone, runner’s high, being unconscious—the lingo is endless. The experience though lives up to the hype. Time slows down, self vanishes, there’s a complete merger between action and awareness.

**The Problem**: The people who want to study Flow states aren’t that good at having them; the people who are really good at having Flow states aren’t all that interested in studying them. As a result, researchers are balkanized, their work occasionally marginalized. There are no coordinated scientific efforts, little cross-pollination of ideas and—as a result—no real roadmap towards discovery and application.

**The Invitation**: If this mission calls to you, and you’d like to join the Project and help us map the genome of Flow, let us know, and welcome to the conversation!​

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2014-04-05T09:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119890>

Take aways from the Q&A...............a shift away from seeing/treating the body as 'object' to feeling/expieriencing it as 'subject', emphasizing feeling rather than knowing, an emphasis on the lived reality rather than the 'after the fact' understood. Kierkegaard said "Life can only be understood backwards but has to be lived forward". Not at all to forget 'understanding' but just to orient oneself in a different direction.

It is something I feel good about and kind of inchoatly felt should, could or might happen. I had 'predicted' (not here) that the logic of Dr Kruse's thinking would take him there and his restless mind would push against and realize it's own limits. True curiosity and not getting stuck in his own 'orthodoxy' but going where the evidence and logic takes one. All in all a good road to take which will go we will see where...............

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-05T21:47:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119936>

Patrick........you like to think metaphysically. You will never solve this riddle this way. But I like this quote you posted: "Life can only be understood backwards but has to be lived forward"

Forget the Patrick way of reading this quote. Embrace the scientist in you and realize what he is trying to convey?

He is describing a thermodynamic problem.

Specifically the second law.........

Now when you are a physicist or an engineer how do you solve a thermodynamics problem?

You read the problem right to left and not left to right.

Now stop.........think where have you heard that advice before?

Which blog?

Then think.....................think deeply and reflect only like a philosopher can.

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-05T22:32:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119945>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=119936)
>
> Patrick........you like to think metaphysically. You will never solve this riddle this way."

Yes!

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=119936)
>
> But I like this quote you posted "Life can only be understood backwards but has to be lived forward"

Oh yeah! This quote is loaded.

> ["Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=119936)
>
> "Forget the Patrick way of reading this quote. Embrace the scientist in you and realize what he is trying to convey?"

I apply the above logic to nearly everything metaphysical I am specifically drawn to. That has really helped me take it to the next level with my perspective and understanding of what is really being conveyed. It's fun and it feels like I am breaking a code. It makes anything metaphysical a lot more interesting and deeper and less fluffy... At least for me it does.

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2014-04-06T00:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119965>

Dr Kruse - thanks for your time and attention. Well the thing is my primary degree is in Philosophy, it's true also I have taken a bunch of classes in chemistry, physiology, biology etc but my main 'training' is in Philosophy. For many years I have considered that a 'waste' but recently I am not so sure. What I mean is 'thinking' is always important and I do believe I have a fairly good grounding in that. But I agree 'thinking' without the fleshing our of science, physics, biology just seems lame. But aside from all that I am who I am, I am 61 y.o. it's too late to change what my whole life's orientation has been. I am not making excuses just stating reality and also accepting myself. It's all I can do or to quote Jeremy I have to start from where I am...........

So that's a kind of 'explanation' for when you say think like a scientist...........well I am NOT a scientist (not really). So I can't or won't pretend to have new and worthwhile things to say about the 2nd Law of Thermodynamics...............however (and maybe kidding myself) I do believe I have some worthwhile things to say coming from a 'philosophical' or 'thinking' perspective and also I have spent over 30 years involved to a greater or lessor degree in 'primal therapy" which would come under the 'psychology' heading though I think it has implications in many areas including for your work at least in my opinion. I am actually fascinated by the possibility of a kind of 'synergy' between your work and Janov's. But no rush that can all wait, I am pretty sure at this point at least you would not be interested in that or vica versa from them. So for the moment I feel a little like the Dutch boy finger in the dyke............. but you have helped me believe in myself for which I am most grateful

You 'challenge' me............thinking right to left which blog? I am not going to 'cheat' just go what's in my head. I can't place a specific blog but you have talked a lot about 'beliefs' how what we don't know generally won't hurt us but what we think we know well could. I agree very much with that. Could that be it? Topological insulators have a different 'quality' on top (electron to elecron communication) verus on 'bottom' protons dropping out. But that's not really is it? Though it also describes 'thinking' I think you are saying. Right to left for some reason I think of the 'reverse electron flow' in mitochondria but that's not a 'good' thing so that's not it. I guess I give up.................a thought can change your DNA..............I am floundering here and in the crossword of life I guess I need more clues. Don't want to put in a wrong word, that just causes more confusion.

### kyrakitty — 2014-04-06T00:40:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119968>

I guess I give up.................a thought can change your DNA..............I am floundering here and in the crossword of life I guess I need more clues. Don't want to put in a wrong word, that just causes more confusion.

In my humble opinion Patrick you sound pretty darned smart and scientisty (I made up this word)!

Definition of scientist from wiki

A **scientist**, in a broad sense, is one engaging in a systematic activity to acquire [knowledge](http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Knowledge) (BOOM!). In a more restricted sense, a scientist is an individual who uses the [scientific method](http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scientific_method).[\[1\]](http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scientist#cite_note-1)

### caroline — 2014-04-06T00:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-your-favorite-march-2013-q-a-take-aways.10444/post-119969>

Patrick - you are such an interesting guy ..... with so much to share. You have a huge knowledge base and you are still a young man .......why are you holding yourself back?
I wish you wouldn't be so pessimistic ....I know you said it is engrained ......but, please .... find a way to Kick it to the curb .......and find the joy in every single second! you live in a gorgeous area and are lucky enough to be going home for the summer.......you should be grinning from ear to ear .......

Do you ever just sit at the beach and watch children play? their joy and curiosity and energy is contagious!

Everyone has had lots of tough bits in their life ......but Jack has said "it is how we carry the load that matters"

I am so grateful that we have gotten a glimpse of Jeremy ...... He has altered my life ..... well, everything here has!

I can't talk philosophy with you ..... or science with Dr. K. - but I can make a difference just by being me .... I see that every single day - in simple ways that I don't even see coming.

My lovely Doctor is so interested in all this and always wants to know more about Jack. The other day he asked me about blogs to read ..... it is a good thing I am never sick - we spend a double appointment talking about all this!
One day I was sitting beside him and we were looking at my labs - he reached out with both hands to check my neck/thyroid ..... I jumped a foot! he had never really touched me before!

Well - he does touch me lightly on the arm when we meet. I asked him about this the other day. I wanted to know if this is instinctive or purposeful? He said it was totally instinctive and so we talked about the quantum magic of touch - I don't think he has ever really thought about it before in that way

### Lahelada — 2014-04-04T17:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-119785>

General question to the panel please.
What do specific food cravings mean? I don't mean the craving for icecream but something like this.

I always had a schizophrenic relationship with mushrooms .I had a great Italian dish ten years ago based solely on mushrooms and I adored it . However it did not change my relationship with mushrooms and I prefer them off my plate rather than on..

FAST FORWARD to today: _I crave mushrooms. What could I be craving ?_
It is getting autumn fall here but I doubt that my internal Rolex has this kind of accuracy yet. Any substances one can think of that I might be short of ? I know context matters etc but a google entry point hint is what I am looking for,please. Thanks

### Hope — 2014-04-04T18:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-119790>

Maybe Vitamin B? Immunity? Selenium?

I know Jack has said he likes them IIRC, because they are one of the few things to eat that haven't changed much over the last few thousands of years or something.

[http://mushroominfo.com/benefits/](http://mushroominfo.com/benefits/)

I'd say mushrooms are a great thing to crave..better than ice-cream or chocolate cake?

### Lahelada — 2014-04-04T18:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-119791>

Thank you . Totally agree on the icecream etc  . Mushrooms are just so outside my normal food spectrum that it arouses my curiosity.

### Lahelada — 2014-04-04T20:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-119806>

For some reason all my reading today leads me to the same place - glutathione. The mushrooms seem to go to the same subject . Pork with garlic and turmeric mushroos tonight. Interesting that favourite flavour combinations have a health effect over and above pleasant taste. Pork or chicken and mushroom is a standard combo throughout the world and the mushrooms aid glutathione production from cysteine...Amazing the things that we know without knowing them.

[http://www.bastyrcenter.org/content/view/1432/](http://www.bastyrcenter.org/content/view/1432/)

### cantweight — 2014-04-04T21:03:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-119814>

I just looked up the seasonality of mushrooms and it said spring/fall. I always love when a craving comes at the right time, like my body's instincts are good on some levels. Every fall I have mad sweet potato cravings, with butter and cinnamon...heaven! In November I have a torrid affair with pomegranates....I walk around with stained finger tips. This winter I have eaten my weight in brussels sprouts and bacon....the whole family has really. And for the past few weeks its been cauliflower. I think most of my cravings having a vitamin A and thyroid connection.

For months I have craved pho. I've had it at least once a week. Extra meat no noodle (ok maybe a few noodles). It invigorates me. Like an elixir! My bone broth is just not the same....will have to work on a recipe

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-04T22:17:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-119826>

mushrooms are a year round food and the only food that has been hardly altered in 6 million yrs. Plus oyster mushrooms are the best for detox because of how they live there life eating worms and recycling there diet into the soil. If you have lyme CE or ME, like some have here, I have recommended shrooms to them.

[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/paul-stamets/oyster-mushroom\_b\_2522084.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/paul-stamets/oyster-mushroom_b_2522084.html)

and one more thing......food cravings are a sign of several key things.......you will learn about in CPC 9

### cantweight — 2014-04-04T22:27:07+0300

### Lahelada — 2014-04-04T23:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-119834>

Now I can't wait for CPC 9 .In the mean time I bought a portobello mushroom AND a large champignon. That is mushroom overkill in my world

Cantweigh, you like Pho and can't really have noodles..Why not use mushrooms, portobellos in strips maybe just lightly cooked instead of noodles and preserve more of whatever they have in goodness that way?

Alternatively stock along the glutathione biosynthesis redox potential increasing lines lines you would need garlic ,onions,turmeric,carrots for betacarotene or palm oil for betacarotene and vtamin E (tocotrienols) If using the palm oil instinctively I would add the oil at the last minute so as not to lose benefits of the tocotrienol. That would be a functional stock I think.

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=119867)
>

I wish we did..... and I am not the only one wishing that

> Any idea if there are plans to build a Wegmans in CT? It's hands down the best grocery store I've ever seen. Produce is terrific, very solid prices, incredible selection (MUCH cheaper on the same organic stuff that [Whole Foods](http://www.city-data.com/knowledge/Whole_foods.html) carries). MA currently has one, and several are opening within the next year.
>
> Our grocery selections are awful:
> \-Stop N' Shop: generally horrid produce, selection is lacking, and prices are somewhat high considering the mediocrity.
> \-[Big Y](http://www.city-data.com/knowledge/Big_Y.html): insanely overpriced, generally caters to a 'less discerning' crowd, lines are ridiculous (no self-checkouts).
> \-Price Chopper: pretty much the same story as S&S, with quality scaled down another notch or two.
> \-Stew Leonards: highly overrated, overpriced and crowded....quality is okay, but selection is lacking
> \-Trader Joes: excellent quality (aside from produce)...limited selection, however.....and only one location beyond Fairfield County.
> \-Whole Foods: obviously the most overpriced of all..and dont really care for the [snob](http://www.city-data.com/knowledge/Snob.html) factor.
>
> Read more: [http://www.city-data.com/forum/connecticut/1923998-wegmans-will-ct-ever-get-one.html#ixzz2y1264YYX](http://www.city-data.com/forum/connecticut/1923998-wegmans-will-ct-ever-get-one.html#ixzz2y1264YYX)

### Tanya — 2014-04-09T21:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-120662>

I am still a novice in mushroom world, but I know enough that usually you don't wash mushrooms, but use a brush to get the dirt out. Oyster mushrooms looked pretty clean to me to begin with, but the label on the package said to wash them prior to use. I did not do that.... Was it a mistake?

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-09T21:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-120666>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=120662)
>
> I am still a novice in mushroom world, but I know enough that usually you don't wash mushrooms, but use a brush to get the dirt out. Oyster mushrooms looked pretty clean to me to begin with, but the label on the package said to wash them prior to use. I did not do that.... Was it a mistake?

Tend to go soggy if you do, don't they?

I usually just wipe them over with a damp piece of kitchen paper (as recommended by Delia Smith).

I suppose sometimes they're grown in questionable mediums  … though some are grown on wood (shitake mushrooms among them I think). I don't worry overmuch, anyway -- they get a wipe and I guess since I usually sauté them in a mixture of butter and olive oil, they get hot enough for any germs on the surface to be killed.

This is one reason why I like them -- besides that they're nutritious (among other things being, IIRC, the only plant-food that has an appreciable amount of vitamin D) and have anti-cancer properties and whatnot -- they will soak up lots of butter.

### cantweight — 2014-04-09T22:04:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-cravings-mushrooms.10445/post-120667>

I do the damp paper towel too. My favorite is sauteed with butter, olive oil, salt and pepper then add red wine and veal demi glace. Oh my lord! mmmm!

I serve it over a nice grass fed steak with a big glass of wine....

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=120753)
